segments.text: 72% of Indian professionals want to change jobs in 2026 and only 16% are prepared to find one. According to a
LinkedIn survey, applicants per open role in India have more than doubled since 2022. 76% of professionals say
finding a role has become harder in just the last year. And it's not because people got worse. It's because the rules changed. Think of it like this. 3 years
ago, you applied, a recruiter screened your resume, and maybe you had two interviews. Today, your resume is screened by AI before any human sees it.
While 87% say they're comfortable using AI at work, most have no idea how AI is being used to screen them out during
recruitment. Even recruiters are struggling. 74% say it's harder to find the right talent, not because talent doesn't exist, but because the matching
process itself is broken. The gap between having skills and demonstrating skills in a way AI systems recognize is massive. What do you think about it?
